# SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Question A1: Conceptual Short-Answer
**[KTU University Exam - June 2023 Model]**

**Q: Define "Effective Span" of a simply supported slab as per IS 456:2000. State its significance in slab design.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

> **Effective Span of a Simply Supported Slab** is defined in IS 456:2000 Cl. 22.2(a) as the **clear span plus the effective depth of the slab**, i.e., $L_{eff} = L_{clear} + d$. However, the effective span shall **not exceed the center-to-center distance** between the supports.
>
> **Significance:**
> 1. The effective span directly affects the **bending moment** ($M_u \propto L^2$) and **shear force** ($V_u \propto L$) calculations.
> 2. It governs the **deflection** of the slab (L/d ratio check).
> 3. A larger effective span leads to a deeper slab section and more reinforcement, impacting cost and structural weight.
> 4. Correctly identifying the effective span ensures the slab design is **safe and economical**.

### Question A2: Conceptual Short-Answer
**[KTU University Exam - December 2022 Model]**

**Q: Differentiate between One-Way and Two-Way Slabs. State the IS 456 criterion for the same.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

| Aspect | One-Way Slab | Two-Way Slab |
|--------|--------------|--------------|
| **Definition** | Slab bending primarily in one direction (short direction) | Slab bending in both directions |
| **IS 456 Criterion** | $L_y / L_x > 2$ | $L_y / L_x \leq 2$ |
| **Load Transfer** | Short direction only → two opposite supports | Both directions → all four supports |
| **Reinforcement** | Main steel in short direction, distribution steel in long direction | Main steel in both directions (with proper curtailment) |
| **Bending Moment** | $M_u = w L_x^2 / 8$ (SS) | Use IS 456 Table 26 coefficients |
| **Design Approach** | Treat as beam of unit width | Use yield line theory or IS coefficients |

> Where $L_x$ = shorter span, $L_y$ = longer span.

## 5.2 Part B Questions (14 Marks Each) — Module Internal Choice

### Question A: Complete G+1 Building Design

**[KTU University Exam - May 2024 Model - 14 Marks]**

> **Q: A double-storied residential building (G+1) has three rooms on each floor. Each room measures 4.0 m × 5.0 m (clear dimensions). The height of each story is 3.0 m. The walls are 230 mm thick brick masonry. Design the following for the building using M20 concrete and Fe 415 steel. SBC of soil is 150 kN/m². Live load on floor is 2 kN/m² and on roof is 1.5 kN/m².**
>
> **(a)** Design the **roof slab** of one of the rooms as a two-way slab. **(7 marks)**
>
> **(b)** Design an **interior column** with its **isolated footing** for the same building. **(7 marks)**

#### Model Solution — Part (a) [7 marks]

**Step 1: Slab Type Identification (1 mark)**
- Lx = 4 m, Ly = 5 m
- Ly/Lx = 5/4 = 1.25 < 2 → **Two-way slab**

**Step 2: Load Calculation (2 marks)**

$$\text{DL: Slab SW} = 0.15 \times 25 = 3.75 \text{ kN/m}^2$$
$$\text{FF} = 1.0 \text{ kN/m}^2, \text{Total DL} = 4.75 \text{ kN/m}^2$$
$$\text{LL} = 1.5 \text{ kN/m}^2, \text{Total} = 6.25 \text{ kN/m}^2$$
$$w_u = 1.5 \times 6.25 = 9.375 \text{ kN/m}^2$$

**Step 3: Design Moments (1 mark)**
$$M_{ux} = 0.062 \times 9.375 \times 4^2 = 9.30 \text{ kN·m/m}$$
$$M_{uy} = 0.062 \times 9.375 \times 5^2 = 14.53 \text{ kN·m/m}$$

**Step 4: Effective Depth (1 mark)**
$$d = \frac{L_x}{20} = \frac{4000}{20} = 200 \text{ mm}$$

**Step 5: Steel Calculation (2 marks)**
$$A_{st,short} = \frac{0.5 \times 20}{415} \left[ 1 - \sqrt{1 - \frac{4.6 \times 9.3 \times 10^6}{20 \times 1000 \times 200^2}} \right] \times 1000 \times 200$$
$$A_{st,short} = 130.6 \text{ mm}^2/\text{m} < A_{st,min} = 240 \text{ mm}^2/\text{m}$$
$$\text{Provide 8 mm Ø @ 200 mm c/c (short span)}$$

$$A_{st,long} = 0.12\% \times 1000 \times 200 = 240 \text{ mm}^2/\text{m}$$
$$\text{Provide 8 mm Ø @ 200 mm c/c (long span)}$$

**Final Slab Design:** **150 mm thick slab, 8 mm Ø @ 200 mm c/c in both directions**

#### Model Solution — Part (b) [7 marks]

**Step 1: Load on Column (1 mark)**

Tributary area = 4 × 5 = 20 m²

For G+1 (3 floors total of slab = GF slab, FF slab, Roof slab):
- Roof slab load: (4.75 + 1.5) × 20 = 125 kN
- Floor slab load: (4.75 + 2.0) × 20 = 135 kN
- Beam self-weight: 2 × 5 × 0.23 × 0.40 × 25 = 23 kN/floor → 46 kN total
- Column self-weight: 0.30 × 0.30 × 6 × 25 = 13.5 kN

$$P_u = 1.5 \times (125 + 135 + 46 + 13.5) = 1.5 \times 319.5 = 479.25 \text{ kN}$$

**Step 2: Column Design (3 marks)**

Adopt 300 mm × 300 mm column, M20, Fe 415
$e_{min} = (3000/500) + (300/30) = 16 \text{ mm} < 20 \text{ mm}$, use 20 mm

$\frac{P_u}{f_{ck} b D} = \frac{479250}{20 \times 300 \times 300} = 0.266$, $e/D = 0.067$

From SP-16: $p = 0.6\% < 0.8\%$ (minimum)

$$A_{st} = 0.008 \times 300 \times 300 = 720 \text{ mm}^2$$
**Provide 4 #16 mm bars (804 mm²), 8 mm ties @ 300 mm c/c**

**Step 3: Footing Design (3 marks)**

$$A_{req} = \frac{319.5}{150} = 2.13 \text{ m}^2 → \text{Adopt } 2.0 \text{ m} \times 2.0 \text{ m}$$

$$w_u = \frac{479250}{4.0} = 119.8 \text{ kN/m}^2$$

$$M_u = 119.8 \times 2.0 \times \frac{(2.0 - 0.3)^2}{2} = 119.8 \times 2.0 \times 1.445 = 346.2 \text{ kN·m}$$

$$d = \sqrt{\frac{346.2 \times 10^6}{0.138 \times 20 \times 2000}} = \sqrt{\frac{346.2 \times 10^6}{5520}} = 250.4 \text{ mm} → \text{Adopt } d = 350 \text{ mm}$$

$$A_{st} = \frac{0.5 \times 20}{415} \left[ 1 - \sqrt{1 - 0.46} \right] \times 2000 \times 350 = 2300 \text{ mm}^2$$

**Provide 12 mm Ø @ 45 mm c/c both ways** in 2.0 m × 2.0 m × 400 mm deep footing

**Footing Punching Shear Check:** (Similar to Section 3.6.5 — verified safe)

---

### Question B: Alternative Choice — Beam and Staircase Design

**[KTU University Exam - May 2024 Model - 14 Marks]**

> **Q: For the same G+1 building described in Question A, design the following:**
>
> **(a)** Design an **interior beam** B1 of span 5 m supporting the slab of one room. The beam is 230 mm wide and is on the perimeter (supports 230 mm wall). **(7 marks)**
>
> **(b)** Design a **dog-legged staircase** connecting the ground floor and first floor. Floor-to-floor height = 3.0 m, rise = 150 mm, tread = 250 mm. **(7 marks)**

#### Model Solution — Part (a) [7 marks]

**Step 1: Loads on Beam (1.5 marks)**

Equivalent UDL from two-way slab:
$$w_{eq} = 9.375 \times \frac{4}{3} \times \left( 3 - 2 \times \frac{4}{5} \right) = 17.5 \text{ kN/m}$$

Wall load = 0.23 × 3.0 × 20 = 13.8 kN/m
Beam self-weight (assume 230 × 550) = 1.725 kN/m
**Total = 33.025 kN/m; Factored $w_u$ = 49.54 kN/m**

**Step 2: BM and SF (1 mark)**

$$M_u = \frac{49.54 \times 5^2}{8} = 154.8 \text{ kN·m}, V_u = 123.85 \text{ kN}$$

**Step 3: Effective Depth (1 mark)**

$$d = \sqrt{\frac{154.8 \times 10^6}{0.138 \times 20 \times 230}} = 495 \text{ mm} → \text{Adopt } d = 500 \text{ mm}$$

**Step 4: Tension Steel (1.5 marks)**

$$A_{st} = \frac{0.5 \times 20}{415} \left[ 1 - \sqrt{1 - 0.619} \right] \times 230 \times 500 = 1062 \text{ mm}^2$$
**Provide 4 #20 mm (1256 mm²) at bottom, 2 #16 mm (402 mm²) at top**

**Step 5: Shear Reinforcement (1 mark)**

$\tau_v = 1.077 \text{ N/mm}^2, \tau_c = 0.66 \text{ N/mm}^2$

**Provide 8 mm Ø 2-legged stirrups @ 200 mm c/c**

**Step 6: Deflection Check (1 mark)**

L/d = 5000/500 = 10 < 31.2 (allowable) ✓

#### Model Solution — Part (b) [7 marks]

**Step 1: Staircase Geometry (1 mark)**

- 10 risers × 150 mm = 1500 mm, Going = 10 × 250 = 2500 mm
- Inclination: $\tan \alpha = 0.6, \alpha = 31°, \cos \alpha = 0.857$
- Effective span: $L_{eff} = 3.5 + 0.5 = 4.0 \text{ m}$
- Waist slab thickness: 150 mm

**Step 2: Loads (1.5 marks)**

$$\text{SW} = \frac{0.15 \times 25}{0.857} = 4.376 \text{ kN/m}^2$$
$$\text{Step weight} = 0.5 \times 0.15 \times 25 = 1.875 \text{ kN/m}^2$$
$$\text{FF} = 1.0 \text{ kN/m}^2, \text{LL} = 2.0 \text{ kN/m}^2$$
$$w_u = 1.5 \times (4.376 + 1.875 + 1.0 + 2.0) = 13.875 \text{ kN/m}^2$$

**Step 3: BM and Effective Depth (1.5 marks)**

$$M_u = \frac{13.875 \times 4^2}{10} = 22.2 \text{ kN·m/m}$$
$$d = \sqrt{\frac{22.2 \times 10^6}{0.138 \times 20 \times 1000}} = 90 \text{ mm} → \text{Adopt } d = 120 \text{ mm}, D = 140 \text{ mm}$$

**Step 4: Steel (1.5 marks)**

$A_{st,req} = 568 \text{ mm}^2/\text{m}$
**Provide 12 mm Ø @ 180 mm c/c (628 mm²/m) as main steel**
**Provide 8 mm Ø @ 250 mm c/c (251 mm²/m) as distribution steel**

**Step 5: Staircase Detailing (1.5 marks)**

- Waist slab thickness 150 mm
- Effective depth d = 120 mm
- Main bars 12 mm Ø at 180 mm c/c, anchored into landing beams
- Distribution bars 8 mm Ø at 250 mm c/c
- Main steel in steps (top) = 12 mm Ø at 180 mm c/c (cranked bars at landing)

## 5.3 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **TOP MARKS-LOSING MISTAKES IN G+1 DESIGN PROBLEMS:**
>
> 1. **Wrong Load Factor**: Students often forget to multiply by 1.5 for load combinations. This error alone can cost **2-3 marks**.
> 2. **Confusion Between Effective Span for Slab and Beam**: Slab uses $L_{clear} + d$ (SS), beam uses center-to-center. Mixing these gives wrong BM.
> 3. **Ignoring Wall Load on Perimeter Beams**: A 230 mm wall on a beam adds **13.8 kN/m** — missing this reduces beam moment by 30%.
> 4. **Using One-Way Slab Formula for Two-Way Slab**: For Lx/Ly > 2 cases, students use $wL^2/8$ incorrectly. IS 456 Table 26 must be used.
> 5. **Not Checking Minimum Steel**: When $A_{st,req} < A_{st,min}$, providing only calculated steel violates IS 456 Cl. 26.5.2.1. **Always check minimum.**
> 6. **Forgetting to Check Punching Shear in Footings**: One-way shear check alone is insufficient. Two-way (punching) shear often governs the depth.
> 7. **Wrong Tributary Area for Columns**: For interior columns, use full panel area (Lx × Ly). For edge columns, use half area in the direction of the edge.
> 8. **Not Detailing Staircase Anchorage**: Staircase main bars must extend into landing beams by development length. Skipping detailing loses 1 mark.
> 9. **Mistaking Footing Depth for Effective Depth**: Total depth D = d + cover + (half bar dia). Using D instead of d for shear/bending checks is a common error.
> 10. **Ignoring Self-Weight of Members**: Beam SW, column SW, and wall SW all add to axial loads. Missing these underestimates column load by 20-30%.

## 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!NOTE]
> **RAPID REVISION CHECKLIST — G+1 Building Design**

### A. Conceptual Foundation
- ✅ G+1 means Ground + First Floor, with **2 intermediate slabs** (GF slab, FF slab/Roof) plus a staircase
- ✅ Three rooms per floor implies **6 enclosed spaces** + staircase, supported by an **RCC frame** (columns + beams + slabs)
- ✅ Design follows **Limit State Method (LSM)** as per **IS 456:2000**

### B. Material Standards (Memorize These!)
- ✅ M20 concrete: $f_{ck} = 20$ N/mm²
- ✅ Fe 415 steel: $f_y = 415$ N/mm²
- ✅ $M_{u,lim} = 0.138 f_{ck} b d^2$
- ✅ Min. steel in slab = 0.12% of bd, in beam = 0.85bd/fy
- ✅ Clear covers: Slab 20 mm, Beam 25 mm, Column 40 mm, Footing 50 mm

### C. Span/Depth Ratios (Basic Values, Fe 415)
- ✅ One-way slab, simply supported: L/d = 20
- ✅ One-way slab, continuous: L/d = 26
- ✅ Two-way slab, simply supported: L/d = 20 (short span)
- ✅ Beam, simply supported: L/d = 20 (basic)

### D. Design Sequence (Cannot Be Reversed)
1. **Slab** → determine d, calculate BM, design main + dist. steel
2. **Beam** → receive slab load + wall load + self-weight, design for BM, SF, deflection
3. **Column** → accumulate axial load from beams, apply min. eccentricity, use SP-16
4. **Footing** → size from SBC, check one-way + two-way shear, design flexural steel
5. **Staircase** → treat as inclined slab, check L/d, design main + dist. steel
6. **Detailing** → BBS, lap lengths, anchorage, curtailable bars

### E. Critical Numerical Checks
- ✅ Effective span (SS slab) = $L_{clear} + d$
- ✅ Effective span (continuous) = center-to-center
- ✅ Two-way slab BM: use IS 456 Table 26 coefficients (αx, αy)
- ✅ Load factor for all combinations = **1.5** (DL + LL)
- ✅ Tributary area for interior column = Lx × Ly (full panel)

### F. Detailing Essentials
- ✅ Laps should be **staggered** (not in one location)
- ✅ L-bend at corners for slab bars: **min. 40 mm or 2× bar dia**
- ✅ Crank bars at supports in slabs (45° crank for ≤ 18 mm bars, 30° for > 18 mm)
- ✅ Hook length = **16φ** for HYSD bars
- ✅ Anchorage length = $L_d/3$ at simply supported end
- ✅ Column ties: spacing ≤ **least of 300 mm, 16φ, b**

### G. Common Pitfalls to Avoid
- ⚠️ Don't use 230 mm brick wall unit weight as 18 kN/m³ — use **20 kN/m³** (IS 875 Part 1)
- ⚠️ Always check **two-way shear in footings** — it usually governs depth
- ⚠️ For columns, use **SP-16 charts** for direct p calculation; don't derive
- ⚠️ Staircase main steel is in the **bottom of waist slab** (tension side at midspan)
- ⚠️ For seismic zone III (Kerala), ensure **ductile detailing** of columns (IS 13920)

### H. Key Equations Summary
$$d = \frac{L_x}{20} \text{ (SS slab)}; M_u = w_u L^2/8 \text{ (SS beam)}; P_u = 1.5(D+L) \cdot A_{trib}$$
$$A_{st} = \frac{0.5 f_{ck}}{f_y} \left[ 1 - \sqrt{1 - \frac{4.6 M_u}{f_{ck} b d^2}} \right] \cdot bd$$
$$A_{footing} = \frac{P}{SBC}; \tau_c = \frac{V_u}{bd}; \tau_{punch} = \frac{V_p}{b_0 d}$$

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **FINAL KTU EXAM STRATEGY:** In a 14-mark design question, allocate time as follows — 2 min for problem reading and data extraction, 3 min for slab design, 3 min for beam design, 3 min for column design, 2 min for footing design, 1 min for staircase (if asked), and 5 min for neat diagrams and reinforcement sketches. **Always draw a labeled reinforcement detail** of the most critical member — this often carries bonus marks.
